sanskrit: हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम् । तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥
hindiTranslation: अगर युद्धमें तू मारा जायगा तो तुझे स्वर्गकी प्राप्ति होगी और अगर युद्धमें तू जीत जायगा तो पृथ्वीका राज्य भोगेगा ।  अतः हे कुन्तीनन्दन तू युद्धके लिये निश्चय करके खड़ा हो जा ॥
englishTranslation: If you are killed, you will attain heaven; if you win, you will enjoy the kingdom. Therefore, rise, Kaunteya, and fight with resolve.
essence: Act now; the result does not cancel the duty.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna is told to stand up and fight without wavering. Whether he wins or dies, he must do what stands before him now.
simpleInsight: Courage grows when the outcome stops being the reason to act.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen while two armies wait. Krishna answers his fear by framing battle as unavoidable duty: death brings one result, victory another, but action itself must still happen.
modernRelevance: You are staring at the message that could end a relationship or save one. Either way, the longer you delay, the heavier it becomes. Krishna’s answer is simple: decide, then stand up.
translationLiteral: हतो (killed) / वा (or) / प्राप्स्यसि (you will attain) / स्वर्गं (heaven) / जित्वा (having won) / वा (or) / भोक्ष्यसे (you will enjoy) / महीम् (the earth) / तस्मात् (therefore) / उत्तिष्ठ (rise) / कौन्तेय (Kaunteya) / युद्धाय (for battle) / कृतनिश्चयः (with firm resolve)
insight: It shifts the mind from outcome anxiety to firm action in the present.

quoteVariants.text: Rise with resolve. Victory or death, the duty stands.
Either way, you were meant to rise.
